messages.content:
Mark and Sarah are purchasing homeowners property insurance. For which one of the following losses would coverage best be provided by their property insurance?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Damage to others’ property for which the insured is legally liable


B

Loss due to death or disability of the named insured


C

Loss of income from loss of business due to economic factors


D

Repair or replacement of the insured’s tangible assets when damaged or destroyed

D

Repair or replacement of the insured’s tangible assets when damaged or destroyed

The Jolly Gift Shop is paying a $200 annual premium to insure its inventory, which has an average value at any time of $40,000. Jolly's premium is calculated per $100 of inventory. What is Jolly's insurance rate?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

$0.05


B

$0.10


C

$0.50


D

$1.00

To calculate Jolly's insurance rate, we need to divide the premium by the value of the inventory and then multiply by 100 to get the rate per $100 of inventory.

Insurance rate = (Premium / Inventory value) * 100

Insurance rate = ($200 / $40,000) * 100

Insurance rate = 0.005 * 100

Insurance rate = 0.5

Therefore, Jolly's insurance rate is $0.50.

The correct answer is C. $0.50.

Insurance customers Winona and Garrett have been advised that for certain homeowners loss exposures they should consider the agreed value method. This method is one in which the insurer and insured agree on the value of property at the time the policy is written. The couple is being advised they will want to consider insuring which one of the following using the agreed value method?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Personal property that is moved from place to place.


B

Vehicles.


C

Fine arts.


D

Older dwellings.

C

Fine arts.

Mackenzie is a corporate underwriter with Greyly Insurance, and Raoul is a field underwriter there. Which one of the following best describes the duties and responsibilities they undertake in these roles?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Raoul was responsible for training Mackenzie.


B

Mackenzie is responsible for arranging appropriate reinsurance.


C

Raoul is responsible for formulating underwriting policy.


D

Mackenzie supports producers, insureds, and the marketing department.

D

Mackenzie supports producers, insureds, and the marketing department.

Liesl and Benjamin have bought their first home and have purchased homeowners insurance through Evert Insurance. In addition to selecting limits and coverages, and customizing their policy, the couple will learn the distinguishing characteristics of insurance policies. For example, they will find out that insurance policies are considered to be contracts of adhesion because

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

The insurer typically has less information about the risk than the insured has.


B

They bind the insurer to a promise to indemnify the insured.


C

Losses covered under them must be fortuitous.


D

The insureds must accept the policy as written with little or no negotiation of terms.

D

The insureds must accept the policy as written with little or no negotiation of terms.

Regulators, in protecting the public interest, are examining Wincox Insurance Company's financial statements. These regulators impose requirements on Wincox's financial statements because of which one of the following?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

They need to make sure that insurers prepare a balance sheet to show their total revenue and expenses.


B

They want to be certain that an insurer does not inflate amounts for loss reserves or surpluses.


C

They do not want insurance companies to overstate their true financial condition.


D

They want to stress that nonadmitted assets are readily convertible into cash.

C

They do not want insurance companies to overstate their true financial condition.

Friends George and Lola are riding in Lola's car with Lola driving. She collides with a parked food truck and does considerable damage to it, but fortunately no one was in the truck at the time. Although her car's front bumper and hood were crushed, Lola is not hurt, but George sustains cuts and bruises and a broken leg. Lola was found legally liable for the accident and has a personal auto insurance policy with Southern Insurers (SI) to cover all the physical damage and liability claims that will arise from this accident. Based on this scenario, which one of the following is true regarding these claimants and their claims against Lola's insurance with SI?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Both the food truck owner and Lola will make first-party claims.


B

Only George will make a third-party claim.


C

Both George and Lola will make first-party claims.


D

Only Lola will make a first-party claim.

B

Only George will make a third-party claim.

An insurance policy is a contract that generally has all of the following distinct characteristics, EXCEPT:

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

The insurance contract is a contract of indemnity.


B

The insurance contract is an unconditional contract.


C

The insurance contract is a contract of utmost good faith.


D

The insurance contract is a nontransferable contract.

B

The insurance contract is an unconditional contract.

Willis is an underwriter with the Grovetown Group. In his role, Willis understands that accurate classification ensures

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

That no premium adjustments will need to be made.


B

A pooling of loss exposures with similar expected loss frequencies and loss severity.


C

Prices higher than competitors' prices.


D

Less monitoring of the underwriting decision.

B

A pooling of loss exposures with similar expected loss frequencies and loss severity.

Mittmore Insurance Company (MIC) is considering one type of marketing system that does not involve MIC's employees. In addition, this marketing system is usually free to work with as many other insurers as needed. This is describing a(n)

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Direct response


B

Exclusive agency


C

Independent agency


D

Direct writer

C

Independent agency

Westmont Insurance Company issues policies for homeowners and personal auto coverages. Which one of the following, in broad terms, states Westmont's promises to the insured?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

The insuring agreement


B

Miscellaneous provisions


C

The definitions


D

The conditions

A

The insuring agreement

A reciprocal insurance exchange is an example of

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

A self-insurance plan.


B

A state insurance program.


C

A federal government insurance program.


D

A private insurer.

A

A self-insurance plan.

John has been an underwriting manager at Westgate Insurance for several years. He is reviewing applications from individuals for whom Westgate recently provided new policies, with these insureds incurring more and larger claims than would be expected based on the information given on their applications. As John discovers this scenario is an example of adverse selection, which one of the following is true?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

An ongoing practice of adverse selection will decrease Westgate’s capacity to write new business.


B

Westgate was charging the insureds higher premiums than appropriate.


C

Westgate was fully aware of the actual risk involved with each of the applicants.


D

An ongoing practice of adverse selection generates profitable business over a long period of time.

A

An ongoing practice of adverse selection will decrease Westgate’s capacity to write new business.

Compulsory auto insurance provides compensation to auto accident victims who might be unable to afford proper medical care or who might be unable to work because of the accident. The benefit derived from insurance in this situation is that of  

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Providing support for the insured's credit.


B

Efficiently utilizing the insured's resources.


C

Promoting risk control activity.


D

Reducing social burden.

D

Reducing social burden.

Patty's vehicle was hit by a truck that failed to stop at an intersection. The police report stated that the driver of the truck was intoxicated. Patty’s insurer paid for the damage to her vehicle and her insurer then assumed Patty’s right to collect damages from the trucker (or the trucker’s insurer) who was responsible for the accident. This is an example of

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Subrogation.


B

Salvage rights.


C

Third party loss settlement.


D

A third-party-over claim.

A

Subrogation.

Which one of the following is true regarding insurer solvency as one of the reasons why insurance is regulated?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Insureds rarely are affected when insurers become insolvent.


B

Insureds can prevent insolvencies by not paying premiums in advance.


C

Sound regulation eliminates insurer insolvencies.


D

Solvency involves the ability to pay losses, even those claimed far into the future.

D

Solvency involves the ability to pay losses, even those claimed far into the future.

Insurers sometimes price their policies too low which drives down price levels within the insurance market. When insurance rate levels become inadequate, some insurers 

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

May receive a bad financial rating. 


B

May withdraw from the market.


C

May move to the surplus lines market.


D

May experience an increase in claims.

B

May withdraw from the market.

David’s television was destroyed in a fire. The television had a useful life of 10 years. The television was purchased three years ago, and its current replacement cost is $1,500. What is the actual cash value of David’s television set?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

$ 450


B

$ 700


C

$1,050


D

$1,200

To calculate the actual cash value (ACV) of David's television set, we need to take into account its depreciation over the past three years.

Depreciation = (Purchase cost / Useful life) * Number of years

Depreciation = ($1,500 / 10) * 3

Depreciation = $450

ACV = Replacement cost - Depreciation

ACV = $1,500 - $450

ACV = $1,050

Therefore, the actual cash value of David's television set is $1,050.

The correct answer is C. $1,050.

Insurance broker Lisa Quinones has a new client. The client has done a poor job of monitoring how well its risk management program has performed. Lisa suggested that as a first step, the client should determine how much was spent on loss control measures, insurance premiums, retained losses, and other risk management efforts. The total value of these expenditures can then be compared to comparable businesses in the same industry sector. The total value of these expenditures is called the

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Cost of risk control.


B

Cost of risk financing.


C

Cost of residual uncertainty.


D

Cost of risk.

D

Cost of risk.

Justine, an underwriter at NewWorld Insurance is working with her company’s premium auditing department. Which one of the following is true regarding this collaboration?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Premium auditors, by observing employees and operations, can provide insights for underwriting the account.


B

A premium audit conducted at the beginning of the policy period can reveal any necessary classification changes.


C

Premium auditors can be particularly helpful with classifying personal accounts rather than commercial ones.


D

Auditors will identify morale hazards such as questionable business practices or a failing business.

A

Premium auditors, by observing employees and operations, can provide insights for underwriting the account.

Sandra is an ambitious producer with the Kramer Group. She is currently working with the Hopper Company, a small manufacturer who wants to explore other insurers but needs help assessing exactly what options are available and what its specific insurance needs are. When Sandra is completing a risk management review with representatives from Hopper, which one of the following can be used as a tool to help her better determine this prospective new customer's insurance needs?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Claims kit


B

Cold canvass


C

Loss run


D

Agency bill

C

Loss run

Leah is an independent agent.  One morning she receives phone calls regarding a billing inquiry, how to file an auto claim, and a request to add an endorsement to an existing policy.  Responding to these phone requests is part of which one of the following functions of an insurance producer?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Sales


B

Risk management review


C

Policy issuance


D

Customer service

D

Customer service

Which one of the following statements is correct with respect to loss exposures and the insurance mechanism?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Insurance is designed primarily to cover loss exposures associated with speculative risks.


B

Most insured loss exposures meet all of the criteria to be ideally insurable.


C

Insurance should ideally cover losses that are definite in time, cause, and location.


D

One purpose of insurance is to enable an insured that has suffered a loss to profit from that loss.

C

Insurance should ideally cover losses that are definite in time, cause, and location.

Which one of the following depends on the risk control function to provide a clear profile of the applicant's loss exposures and related hazards?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Claims


B

Underwriting


C

Marketing and sales


D

Accounting

B

Underwriting

Bill Harmon has just received his new insurance policy from his insurer. As he reviews it for coverages and details, which one of the following will Bill find is true regarding an insurance policy?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

The first page of an insurance policy is usually the declarations.


B

A policy cannot have more than one insuring agreement.


C

Exclusions give details on what the insurer promises to cover.


D

The definitions section is usually located in the middle of a policy.

A

The first page of an insurance policy is usually the declarations.

Hilda wants to operate a floral shop but she is worried that a fire or wind loss could bankrupt her. If Hilda purchases insurance for her shop she will be able to transfer much of the financial risk to an insurer, which will contribute to the growth of her flower shop. The benefit Hilda derives from purchasing insurance is that this will allow Hilda to

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Efficiently use her resources.


B

Obtain credit.


C

Implement loss control in her shop.


D

Guarantee her business's profitability.

A

Efficiently use her resources.

Which one of the following sets of provisions form the core of every insurance policy?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

The definitions, insuring agreement, and declarations


B

The declarations, exclusions, and insuring agreement


C

The insuring agreement, exclusions, and conditions


D

The declarations, definitions, and exclusions

C

The insuring agreement, exclusions, and conditions

Based in Lincoln Nebraska, Eddie's Famous Pizza has recently opened 3 locations in Myrtle Beach, South Carolina.  Not accustomed to having issues obtaining property insurance in the Midwestern United States, Eddie's Famous Pizza has obtained no interest from the private market for coverage on its three Myrtle Beach locations given the windstorm damage potential.  The owner of Eddie's believes he is eligible for a beach and windstorm plan offered by the state government.  What criterion is Eddie leaning on which would prove his point?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

He was not able to obtain coverage in the private market.


B

He is not a fan of insurance pools.


C

The type of business he operates.


D

He is new to Myrtle Beach and has no previous loss history.

A

He was not able to obtain coverage in the private market.

Framers Insurance Company's financial statements for last year provide the following information:

 	 	Earned premiums                                          
$
2,500,000
 	 	Written premiums
$
2,400,000
 	 	Incurred underwriting expenses
$
480,000
 	 	Incurred losses (including loss expenses)	$
2,000,000
Which one of the following was Framers' expense ratio last year?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

.16 (16%)


B

.20 (20%)


C

.30 (30%)


D

.33 (33%)

To calculate the expense ratio, we need to divide the incurred underwriting expenses by the earned premiums:

Expense ratio = Incurred underwriting expenses / Earned premiums

Expense ratio = $480,000 / $2,500,000

Expense ratio = 0.192

This is equivalent to 19.2%, which means the expense ratio for Framers Insurance Company last year was 19.2%.

The closest option to this answer is B. 0.20 (20%).

The correct answer is B. 0.20 (20%).

After consulting with Walt Hamilton, the insurance agent handling its account, Wylie Manufacturing Company set the following goals for the coming year: to reduce workers compensation costs by 10% and to reduce physical damage losses at the manufacturing facility by 15%. These goals are examples of  

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Results standards.


B

Risk transfer standards.


C

Profitability standards.


D

Activity standards.

A

Results standards.

Court awarded general damages are intended to compensate the injured party for

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Hospital bills.


B

Lost wages.


C

Pain and suffering.


D

Lawyers’ fees.

C

Pain and suffering.

Which one of the following is found on the balance sheet of a property and liability insurer?

Available answer options
Select only one option

A

Loss and loss expense reserve


B

Taxes and fees


C

Incurred losses


D

Earned Premiums

A

Loss and loss expense reserve
